tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: South Africa Unites to Fulfill Every Dream on Nine-Year-Old's Bucket List in 12 Hours
stories.summary: An entire community rallied to make all of Zelnay's wishes come true in a single day of pure love and generosity.

stories.headline: Children With Disabilities Play on Major League Diamond, Welcomed as Stars by White Sox
stories.summary: Young athletes with special needs experienced the magic of a big league field, cheered on by professional players who showed them baseball's true heart.

stories.headline: Golden Retriever's Daily 'Kissing Booth' Spreads Neighborhood Joy, One Free Hug at a Time
stories.summary: A Waltham woman opens her window daily so neighbors can get free hugs and licks from her golden retriever, brightening every passing day.

stories.headline: Pigs Walk Again After Experimental Gel Heals Severed Spinal Cords—New Hope for Paralyzed Humans
stories.summary: Led by Michael Lebenstein-Gumovski, scientists in Russia reconnected severed spinal cords in pigs using a fusogen gel, restoring their ability to walk.

stories.headline: Local Glass Company Installs $3,000 Bay Window for Free, Warming a Family's Home and Heart
stories.summary: Todd Embley's KT Fairglass installed a $3,000 bay window for free on 43rd Street, helping homeowner Gordon Passmore end drafty heat loss in his home.

stories.headline: Critically Endangered Marmots Surge to Highest Numbers in 40 Years, 4 Released Into the Wild
stories.summary: Vancouver Island marmots surged from fewer than 30 to over 400—the highest in 40 years—with 40 released this summer by the Marmot Recovery Foundation.

stories.headline: Curaçao's Goalkeeper Delivers Masterclass, Secures Nation's Historic First World Cup Point
stories.summary: Goalkeeper Eloy Room delivered a masterclass, leading Curaçao to a historic 0-0 draw with Ecuador to secure the nation's first-ever World Cup point.

stories.headline: Scientists Design 'Nanotrap' That Catches HIV and Triggers Immune Response—A Step Toward Vaccine
stories.summary: Researchers from Drexel University and University of Pennsylvania created a biological 'nanotrap' using liposomes to catch HIV and trigger immune response.

stories.headline: Kordofan Giraffes Rebound Fivefold in DRC After Poaching Stopped—From 22 to 105 in 14 Years
stories.summary: Kordofan giraffes in Garamba National Park surged from 22 to 105 since 2012, with zero poaching recorded since 2017 thanks to strengthened protection.
